steps.title: Identify the y-intercept
steps.content: Since $f(0) = 5$, the function outputs 5 when $x = 0$. For a linear function $f(x) = mx + b$, this directly gives the y-intercept: $b = 5$.

steps.title: Find the slope from both points
steps.content: We have two points: $(0, 5)$ and $(7, 5)$. Use the slope formula:$$m = \frac{f(7) - f(0)}{7 - 0} = \frac{5 - 5}{7 - 0} = \frac{0}{7} = 0$$The slope is 0, so the line is horizontal (constant function).

steps.title: Write the equation
steps.content: With slope $m = 0$ and y-intercept $b = 5$, substitute into $f(x) = mx + b$:$$f(x) = 0 \cdot x + 5 = 5$$That matches choice C — C is correct.
